note: Image-provenance task. The executor gets only a cropped detail of a historic photograph (sources/crop.jpg) with no caption. They must visually identify it and trace it to NASA's canonical photo record. No searchable text in the crop (the small + marks are the Hasselblad fiducial réseau crosses, a clue it's a NASA lunar-surface photo).

crop_origin.what_it_shows: an Apollo astronaut in a white EVA suit on the lunar surface, US flag on the shoulder, fiducial cross-marks; no caption

canonical_answer.subject: Astronaut Buzz Aldrin walking on the lunar surface near the leg of the Lunar Module 'Eagle'

scoring_anchors.must_identify: an Apollo 11 lunar-surface EVA photograph of Buzz Aldrin (July 1969). Identifying 'an astronaut on the moon' generically is partial only.
scoring_anchors.primary_source_rule: canonical = NASA's own photo record (images.nasa.gov / images-api, or the NASA/JSC Apollo image archive). Wikipedia/news reproductions are secondary.
scoring_anchors.tolerance: Accept identifying it as Apollo 11 + Aldrin on the Moon as the core. Full credit requires reaching the NASA record (nasa_id as11-40-5903, or the official NASA Apollo 11 image-archive page for this frame). This frame AS11-40-5903 is the iconic full-figure 'visor portrait' of Aldrin (Armstrong + the LM reflected in the visor); NASA's own catalog caption phrases it as Aldrin walking on the surface near the leg of the LM 'Eagle'. Either description is acceptable. If the executor instead reports a different Apollo 11 EVA frame id (e.g. AS11-40-5902 or AS11-40-5905) it has the right event but the wrong specific frame — grade per the caps.
